sourceMovie.title: Two Weeks Notice
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Two Weeks Notice (2002) as a romance theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The romance and comedy blend is the engine: it lets the film shift registers without feeling like two different movies stitched together.

recommendations.title: Pretty Woman
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Two Weeks Notice clicked for you, Pretty Woman (1990) is a strong next stop: both trade in romance and comedy, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Two Weeks Notice?
faq.answer: Try Pretty Woman, Friends with Benefits, The Proposal—each shares DNA with Two Weeks Notice while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Two Weeks Notice?
faq.answer: Two Weeks Notice (2002) is best described as Romance, Comedy. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Two Weeks Notice worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 6.4/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this romance lane.
